reason_for_decision: The article clearly explains RF geolocation as an emitter-location problem, separates it from Remote ID and simple detection, and gives enough method and limitation context to pass without revision.
strengths: The article clearly separates detection, geolocation, and pilot-position interpretation rather than blurring them together.
It gives beginners a usable explanation of why AOA, TDOA, and hybrid methods exist together.
The limits section correctly narrows the promise of pilot positioning by emphasizing emitter origin, confidence, and workflow context.
summary_assessment: This is a strong Foundation article: it is readable, technically grounded, and useful enough to pass without revision.
